html:
Marble Pillars are naturally spawning Structures found around the Wooden Thing in a Chess biome in Sandbox Mode or in various Chess biomes in Adventure Mode. Usually, there are Clockwork Monsters and Evil Flowers near them. They can be mined with a Pickaxe or the Pick/Axe to acquire 2-3 Marble pieces (2 + 33% chance to get 1 extra). The pickaxe requires 10 hits to break a Marble Pillar down, whereas the Pick/Axe only requires 8. Though it may use a lot of durability off a pickaxe, it is recommended to mine Marble Pillars so the player can make a Marble Suit for fighting.

Trivia

  • Marble Pillars were introduced in the A Little Rain Must Fall update. 
  • Marble Pillars used to spawn around Maxwell's Door in the old versions of Don't Starve.

Gallery

  • The three stages of a broken down pillar.
  • Wilson using his Pick/Axe on a Marble Pillar.
  • An older version of Don't Starve showing Maxwell's Door on Carpeted Flooring surrounded by Marble Pillars.
  • Marble pillars as they appear on the map.
  • An X with 4 marble pillars and several Clockwork Knights.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

"marblepillar"
“I think I could use that.”
–Wilson
“I wonder if the rest burnt down.”
–Willow
“Is too heavy to lift.”
–Wolfgang
“Even stone can not withstand the constant pull of decay.”
–Wendy
“CRUMBLING REMAINS. HAH.”
–WX-78
“It shows the touch of a Roman artisan. How amazing.”
–Wickerbottom
“Somebody must have built that.”
–Woodie
“I've always wondered who built those.”
–Maxwell
“I would very much like to talk to the architect of that structure.”
–Wagstaff
“Fit för a palace!”
–Wigfrid
“Nothing lasts forever in this world.”
–Webber
“What was it meant to hold up?”
–Walani
“I wonder how many counter tops I could get out of this...”
–Warly
“A marble peg leg. For a giant.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS LOG O' ROCK”
–Wilba
“Solid”
–Wormwood
“A perfectly good marble column just sitting there for anyone to take.”
–Wheeler
Marble Pillars are naturally spawning Structures found around the Wooden Thing in a Chess biome in Sandbox Mode or in various Chess biomes in Adventure Mode. Usually, there are Clockwork Monsters and Evil Flowers near them. They can be mined with a Pickaxe or the Pick/Axe to acquire 2-3 Marble pieces (2 + 33% chance to get 1 extra). The pickaxe requires 10 hits to break a Marble Pillar down, whereas the Pick/Axe only requires 8. Though it may use a lot of durability off a pickaxe, it is recommended to mine Marble Pillars so the player can make a Marble Suit for fighting.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Marble Pillars were introduced in the A Little Rain Must Fall update.
Marble Pillars used to spawn around Maxwell's Door in the old versions of Don't Starve.

'''Marble Pillars''' are naturally spawning [[Structures]] found around the [[Wooden Thing]] in a [[Chess]] [[biome]] in [[Sandbox Mode]] or in various Chess [[biome]]s in [[Adventure Mode]]. Usually, there are [[:Category:Clockwork Monsters|Clockwork Monsters]] and [[Evil Flower]]s near them. They can be mined with a [[Pickaxe]] or the [[Pick/Axe]] to acquire 2-3 [[Marble]] pieces (2 + 33% chance to get 1 extra). The pickaxe requires 10 hits to break a Marble Pillar down, whereas the Pick/Axe only requires 8. Though it may use a lot of durability off a pickaxe, it is recommended to mine Marble Pillars so the player can make a [[Marble Suit]] for fighting.

=={{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* Marble Pillars were introduced in the ''[[Version History#March 12, 2013 - A Little Rain Must Fall|A Little Rain Must Fall]]'' update. 
* Marble Pillars used to spawn around [[Maxwell's Door]] in the old versions of ''Don't Starve''.
